title: 続きを進めて。kernelで進めて。 コスト前提を設計に入れます。契約済みの Claude / GPT / GLM を通常ループの主 軸にし、Copilot を低コスト補助、Gemini と xAI は overflow と tie-break に 限定する方針で /kernel 契約を更新します。 • Updated Plan └ Adding a cost-aware redesign layer to /kernel based on the user's subscription and metered-provider constraints. I will encode provi...
summary_text: 1. High: aggregate crash still leaves no durable council failure snapshot, so `vote -> implement` continuity is not replay-safe on the exact failure seam we were hardening. The helper only writes [`council-status.json`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh#L210) after [`aggregate_script` succeeds](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh#L195) and after [`integration-vars.sh` is sourced](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh#L199). I simulated an aggregate crash with a stubbed `FUGUE_AGGREGATE_VOTES_SCRIPT`; result was `rc=23` and `status_present=false`. Current regression only covers the normal pass/fail aggregate cases in [`tests/test-kernel-council-review.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-kernel-council-review.sh#L169), not aggregate crash. 2. Medium: the canonical readiness gate is still not self-contained. [`scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L5) depends on an absolute path outside the repo: `/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/tests/test-codex-prompt-launcher.sh`. On a clean machine or different checkout layout, the repo can be healthy and the readiness gate can still fail for environmental reasons. 3. Medium: the canonical readiness gate still does not exercise live launcher/runtime smoke, only static and unit-style checks. [`scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L20) through [L33] run prompt checks, continuity/unit tests, and the launcher test harness, but not actual `kernel SMOKE_RESULT_MARKER=...` / `vote SMOKE_RESULT_MARKER=...` live command execution. That means transport/bootstrap regressions can still slip past `kernel-vote-readiness: PASS`. 4. Medium: the new canonical readiness gate is not itself wired into the automatic enforcement surfaces. The harness hook only auto-runs the narrower council unit test in [`scripts/harness/fugue-harness-hook.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/fugue-harness-hook.sh#L76), not the full readiness gate. Likewise, the orchestration workflow path list in [`fugue-orchestration-gate.yml`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L5) does not include `scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh`. So the “final completion gate” can drift without being enforced by hook/CI. Assumptions: - I treated “problem points” as defects or blind spots proven by simulation or direct code inspection, not speculative architecture concerns. - The strongest concrete evidence is the reproduced aggregate-crash case; the other three are enforcement/readiness blind spots rather than immediate runtime breakage.
